localizations.title: The Nautilus' Traps
localizations.hiddenObject_1: You must search the cave and look for the 4 numbers. One of them is on the fish on the right.
localizations.code_1111: You get a few extra minutes of oxygen! Look carefully throughout the Nautilus and maybe you'll find more scuba tanks.
localizations.code_3764: You worm your way through a maze of narrow caves. Light, at last! You see the surface!
localizations.code_4444: You get a few more minutes of oxygen!
localizations.code_5476: The panel opens. You find a manometer.
localizations.code_7745: The notes trigger the opening of a hidden panel.
localizations.code_9999: You get a few more minutes of oxygen!
localizations.code_2284: There's no code to enter for the time being.
localizations.code_7050: The opening of this hatch doesn't seem to require a code.
localizations.hint_12: The technical data of the Nautilus should be useful. Think about the "minus"
localizations.hint_3: Each stave has a note to be played on the organ.
localizations.hint_5: You must take these four books.
localizations.hint_7_first: To choose the right notes, you must have a sheet music and one of the books from the bookcase.
localizations.hint_7: Each note identified in the book matches a number on the organ.
localizations.hint_9: This book will definitely help you choose the right notes in the organ sheet music.
localizations.hint_10_first: 4 of these numbers will be clearly pointed out by the lantern.
localizations.hint_10: Place the lantern card ABOVE this one. Keep shifting it until each beam of light perfectly reaches a number.
localizations.hint_13: You need a tool to open it.
localizations.hint_16_first: The missing words on the plate are the key to obtain the 4-digit code: how "TO" survive…
localizations.hint_16: How "TO" survive… In the "FORe"… "SomeONE"... "SIXains". Now, you have the 4-digit code.
localizations.hint_19_first: The pitch of the sounds is the key. There are 4 beeps and 4 columns with these words.
localizations.hint_19: A high-pitched sound indicates the top line and a low-pitched sound indicates the bottom line. The first beep is neither low-pitched nor high-pitched…
localizations.hint_20_first: First, you must repair the electric panel to find the code.
localizations.hint_20: Place the wires exactly as shown on card 41, turn them all the while you superimpose them. Thus, each symbol will match its number.
localizations.hint_22: There is a monster behind. Better not antagonize it…
localizations.hint_27_first: You must superimpose the wires to form 4 unbroken lines.
localizations.hint_27: Place the wires exactly as shown on card 41, turn them all the while you superimpose them. Thus, each symbol will match its number.
localizations.hint_29: Alone, it's worthless. When paired, its needle will point to a number.
localizations.hint_30: Useful to open stubborn doors and to unscrew.
localizations.hint_33: You must connect one wire of card 27 to each connector.
localizations.hint_34: You must connect one wire of card 74 to each connector.
localizations.hint_37_first: The missing words on the plate are the key to obtain the 4-digit code: how "TO" survive…
localizations.hint_37: How "TO" survive… In the "FORe"… "SomeONE"... "SIXains". Now, you have the 4-digit code.
localizations.hint_40: The technical data of the Nautilus should be useful. Think about the "minus"
localizations.hint_41: You must reconstruct this machine. Watch closely the numbers position, it will help you.
localizations.hint_49: The lantern, when properly positioned, will provide 4 numbers. The door provides the sequence.
localizations.hint_52: Better to stop here. It looks dangerous!
localizations.hint_54: Alone, it's worthless. When paired, its needle will point to a number.
localizations.hint_55_first: Use it to electrify the hull. Turn each button in the right position to find out which card to take.
localizations.hint_55: Follow the instructions of the handbook in the library. Turn the letters and you'll obtain numbers and symbols. Then, do the math to find a card.
localizations.hint_61: It's great, but you haven't much oxygen. Better look elsewhere in the cave.
localizations.hint_65_first: The screen shows, in sequence, how to position the W, X, Y, and Z manometers.
localizations.hint_65: Place the cards using the white marks. Follow the needle and each time, you will find a number.
localizations.hint_72: You must follow the instructions to the letter when you get the world map entitled "First Trip".
localizations.hint_74_first: You must superimpose the wires to form 4 unbroken lines.
localizations.hint_74: Place the wires exactly as shown on card 41, turn them all the while you superimpose them. Thus, each symbol will match its number.
localizations.hint_75_first: Follow an accurate itinerary with the dividers to obtain a 4-digit code.
localizations.hint_75: Never make land and remember that the last trip is shorter.
localizations.hint_79: These dividers are well fit for following a course on a world map. But there are several maps on board of the Nautilus.
localizations.hint_88_first: The lantern, when properly positioned, will provide 4 numbers.
localizations.hint_88: Place the lantern card ABOVE card 10. Keep shifting it until each beam of light perfectly reaches a number.
localizations.hint_91: The 4 positions of the altimeter are the solution. Pay attention to Alphonse's message.
localizations.hint_92: Alone, it's worthless. When paired, its needle will point to a number.
localizations.hint_95_first: You must position the large gear without numbers. Each gear will turn and point to a number.
localizations.hint_95: A gear changes the next one's direction of rotation. A belt keeps the direction of the following one. The large gear must turn by two pegs to the left.
localizations.hint_99: Alone, it's worthless. When paired, its needle will point to a number.
localizations.machine_19: A compartment opens in the sonar!
